page: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware support for AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AES cryptography.

The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 1843 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

Benchmark data indicates solid performance across standard operations. The server achieves a Passmark CPU score of 48717.7 and a Passmark memory score of 2757.12, which places its memory performance in the top 25% tier. It demonstrates strong capabilities in cryptographic operations, with a Passmark encryption score of 43321.1, and image processing, scoring 14001.0 on Geekbench. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, it achieves a strong text generation speed of 275.95 tokens per second. However, for larger workloads like LLM inference with a 70B model, performance drops to a weak 2.74 tokens per second for text generation, highlighting a clear tradeoff for large-scale AI workloads.

Given its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local storage capacity, the D48ds_v5 offers a cost-efficient profile for resource-dense general-purpose workloads without the overhead of specialized GPU hardware. It is highly suitable for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 2.8 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and a bundled 1843 GB local disk with Premium Storage capability. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers balanced performance, highlighted by strong memory and encryption benchmark results. It achieves a Passmark memory score of 2757.12 and a Passmark encryption score of 43321.1, both placing in the top 25% tier. While it handles smaller LLM inference tasks efficiently, performance drops significantly on larger 70B models. The D48ds_v5 is well-suited for database operations, software compilation, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and a 1843 GB local disk, optimized for databases, web serving, and general enterprise workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high memory bandwidth, and substantial local SSD storage.
